title: Everyday Slang
description: The casual, current expressions young Spanish speakers actually use with friends every day.
situation: Sounding natural and relaxed - greetings, reactions, and filler you would hear hanging out with friends.

phrases.tip: The everyday casual greeting. Drop the 'hola' and just lead with this
The classic Spain word for cool. In Mexico say 'chido', in Argentina 'copado'
Mexican equivalent of 'guay'. '¡Qué chido!' = how cool!
Literally 'uncle/aunt', but in Spain it just means 'mate'. Very common with friends
Literally 'how strong'. A reaction to shocking or wild news, good or bad
Literally 'I'm without a white one' - an old coin. Means you have no money
Spain slang. 'Me mola' = I'm into it. '¡Mola mucho!' = it's really cool
An admiring reaction to something impressive. Playful and positive
Playful, mostly Spain. '¿Estás de cachondeo?' = are you kidding me?
The default verb for making plans. '¿Quedamos el sábado?' = shall we hang out Saturday?
Literally 'I'm turned to dust'. Use after a long day or a hard workout
The Spain go-to for 'OK'. Heard constantly. In Latin America use 'dale' or 'va'
'Estoy flipando' = I can't believe it. Can be good or bad shock. Very Spain
Slang for working a job. 'El curro' = the job or the office. Mostly Spain
Mildly cheeky but not rude. A firm, playful refusal among friends
Hype and encouragement. Shout it to pump someone up or cheer them on
